title: Pattern blocks / geometric shapes (angle + edge accuracy)
context: Pattern blocks (hexagon, trapezoid, rhombus, triangle, square, narrow rhombus) are used for geometry, fractions, and spatial reasoning. Their critical constraint is angle accuracy: the 60-degree equilateral triangle must have exact 60-degree angles; the hexagon must be a regular hexagon; edges shared by complementary shapes must be exactly equal length so the shapes tile correctly. An angle error of even a few degrees will prevent the shapes from tiling and teaches a false geometric relationship.
objective: Implement a pattern-blocks / geometric-shapes generator module that produces geometrically exact shapes with verified angle and edge accuracy, reviewed by a math educator.
acceptanceCriteria: Generates the six standard pattern-block shapes: regular hexagon, trapezoid (half hexagon), wide rhombus (two equilateral triangles), equilateral triangle, square, and narrow rhombus (30-60-90).
All angles are exactly correct to within the measurement precision of the verification harness; shared edge lengths are equal within tolerance, so shapes tile correctly.
The verification harness is extended to check angle accuracy (interior angles of each polygon) and shared-edge length equality.
Math-educator review sign-off for geometric and pedagogical correctness recorded.
All standard variants generated (A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print).
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-002.json -- verification harness (must extend to check angles)
output: A pattern-blocks generator module integrated into the engine package, with a spec file and example output PDFs showing correct tiling, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
